zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Williamson於1603年12月19日出生在英國爾維爾姆斯洛福時，他的父親Nicholas Williamson28歲，母親Lady Isabell Bradshaw14歲。他於1627年1月7日與Jane Harrope在英國爾維爾姆斯洛福結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1698年在美國維吉尼亞州易斯爾 本 懷特易斯爾 本 懷特享年95歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Williamson于1603年12月19日出生在英国爾維爾姆斯洛福时，他的父亲Nicholas Williamson28岁，母亲Lady Isabell Bradshaw14岁。他于1627年1月7日与Jane Harrope在英国爾維爾姆斯洛福结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1698年在美国弗吉尼亚州易斯爾 本 懷特易斯爾 本 懷特享年95岁。

nameMeanings.name: Williamson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northern) and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name William + patronymic -son ‘son of William’. This surname is also found in Northern Ireland (Ulster), especially in Antrim.
Americanized form of Norwegian, Danish, and North German Wilhelmsen , Swedish Wilhelmsson, or Dutch and North German Willemsen , all cognates of 1.

History: This surname was brought to North America from northern England and northern Ireland independently by numerous different bearers from the 17th century onward. Timothy Williamson is recorded in Plymouth Colony in 1643.

variants.name: Willamson
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Williamson  .

variants.name: William
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Simon , now rare, but more common in the Middle Ages, when it gave rise to the surnames Simms and Simpson.

variants.name: Simon
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Williamson was born on 19 December 1603, in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England, his father, Nicholas Williamson, was 28 and his mother, Lady Isabell Bradshaw, was 14. He married Jane Harrope on 7 January 1627, in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. He died in 1698, in Isle of Wight, Isle of Wight, Virginia, United States, at the age of 95.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Williamson(이)가 1603년 12월 19일 영국 체실르 윌슬로에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nicholas Williamson(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Isabell Bradshaw(은)는 14세였다. 그는 1627년 1월 7일 영국 체실르 윌슬로에서 Jane Harrope(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1698년 미국 버지니아 주 이슬일 프 와이트 이슬일 프 와이트에서 95세의 나이로 사망했다.
